web.markdown:
# Recipe Scaler

This handy recipe scaler allows you to easily divide or multiply your favorite recipes. Just copy and paste the original recipe ingredients into the textbox below, determine your conversion factor, and click the "Convert" button to get the new scaled recipe. It's that easy!

Recipe Scaler \| Recipe Converter

Original Number of Servings:

Needed Number of Servings:

OR

Scale:\- Custom Factor -Quarter (¼)Third (⅓)Half (½)One and One-Half (1 ½)Double (2x)Triple (3x)Quadruple (4x)

Conversion Factor (CF):

Results

Conversion Factor: **0.5**

Original Recipe Ingredients  Try a Sample Recipe

New Scaled Recipe

Round to nearest fraction (nearest 1/8th)

## Scaling a Recipe Up or Down

To scale your recipe up or down:

1\. Find the recipe conversion factor (CF).

CF = N / O

Where:

**CF** = conversion factor,

**N** = new recipe amount (or yield),

**O** = original (or old) recipe yield.

2\. Multiply each ingredient amount by the CF.

You may also be interested in our [Cooking Measurement Conversion Calculator](https://goodcalculators.com/cooking-conversion-calculator/ "Cooking Measurement Conversion Calculator") or [Cake Pan Conversion Calculator](https://goodcalculators.com/cake-pan-converter/ "Cake Pan Conversion Calculator")

# Recipe Scale Conversion Calculator – Recipe Multiplier

If you find the process of scaling a recipe up or down confusing or tedious like we do, then you’re going to love this recipe scaling calculator!

Enter your ingredients, and the calculator will increase or decrease them automatically.

## Cite This Page

Sexton, J. (n.d.). [Recipe Scale Conversion Calculator – Recipe Multiplier](https://www.inchcalculator.com/recipe-scale-conversion-calculator/). Inch Calculator. Retrieved April 28, 2026, from https://www.inchcalculator.com/recipe-scale-conversion-calculator/

## How to Scale a Recipe

If you are a professional chef looking to scale up a recipe for an event or a home cook looking to scale down a family recipe, this calculator will give you a step-by-step process that guarantees accurate results every time.

![infographic showing how to adjust the amount of ingredients to scale a recipe up or down.](https://www.inchcalculator.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/11/scale-recipe-infographic.png)

### Step One: Find the Conversion Factor

Firstly, determine if you are scaling up or down in terms of portion size per person. Once you’ve determined portion size, use this formula to find the conversion factor.

conversion factor = portions needed ÷ recipe portions

Industry pros call the number of portions the _yield_, so the conversion factor here is the required yield divided by the recipe’s yield. Doubling the recipe means the conversion factor is 2, and cutting the recipe in half would be 0.5.[\[1\]](https://ecampusontario.pressbooks.pub/basickitchenandfoodservicemanagement/chapter/convert-and-adjust-recipes-and-formulas/)

Hint: you can use a tool such as our [scale calculator](https://www.inchcalculator.com/scale-calculator/) to find the scale factor.

### Step Two: Adjust the Recipe Using the Conversion Factor

Using the conversion factor, you can easily adjust the amount of each ingredient in your recipe by multiplying the portion size by the conversion factor.

For example, if the conversion factor is 2 and the recipe calls for 3 tablespoons of an ingredient, the scaled recipe calculation would be 2 × 3 = 6, which equals 6 tablespoons.

### Step Three: Convert Measurements

Finally, convert the measurements into their new quantity. This step is optional but greatly simplifies your recipe, and it’s what the real chefs do! If a recipe calls for 4 ounces, but the final adjusted measurement is 16 ounces, then you’d convert that 16 ounces into 1 pound.

When you scale a recipe, the measures used might not be appropriate any longer. If a recipe calls for 4 ounces and the recipe is quadrupled, then the new amount would be 16 ounces. Since there are 16 ounces in a pound, you can convert the ingredient quantity from 16 ounces to 1 pound.

Refer to our [cooking conversion calculator](https://www.inchcalculator.com/cooking-conversion-calculator/) or the common list of kitchen conversions below for quick and easy conversions.

| Measurement | Equivalent Measurement |
| --- | --- |
| 1 tablespoon | 3 teaspoons |
| 1 fluid ounce | 2 tablespoons |
| 1 cup | 8 fluid ounces |
| 1 pint | 2 cups |
| 1 quart | 2 pints |
| 1 gallon | 4 quarts |
| 1 pound | 16 ounces |

Conversions of common kitchen and baking measurements

Try our [volume](https://www.inchcalculator.com/convert/volume/) and [weight](https://www.inchcalculator.com/convert/weight/) conversion calculators to simplify converting from a smaller unit to a larger one.

## Things to Consider When Scaling a Recipe

Here are some common things to consider when scaling recipes to ensure successful results anytime you want to convert a recipe.

### Measuring by Weight vs. Volume

Scaling up in volume increases the inaccuracy of measuring by volume. Converting to weight first decreases the margin of error. See our [sugar](https://www.inchcalculator.com/convert/sugar/) and [flour](https://www.inchcalculator.com/convert/flour/) conversion calculators to convert from volume to weight.

Dry ingredients should be measured by weight rather than volume to improve the accuracy of the recipe. Dry ingredients vary slightly in density and can be compacted differently when measuring.

PRO TIP: Never scoop dry ingredients like flour or powdered sugar directly from the bag with a measuring up as it’s settled and compacted. Use a whisk to aerate dry ingredients before measuring for more accurate results.

Professional chefs and bakers recommend that dry ingredients should be measured by weight instead of volume.[\[2\]](https://www.nist.gov/pml/owm/culinary-measurement-tips) Convert to weight before using the calculator above.

PRO TIP: A small digital kitchen scale is a valuable kitchen tool if you bake a lot.

### Cooking and Baking Times May Change

Be aware that converting and scaling recipes likely means cooking/baking times and temperatures will require adjusting as well. It’s wise to practice with a recipe or two from start to finish so you can record the new recipe yields and any other cooking or baking notes that arise (pun intended).

## Blender Icon Similar Cooking Resources

- [Butter Conversion CalculatorsChevron Right Icon](https://www.inchcalculator.com/convert/butter/)
- [Flour Conversion CalculatorsChevron Right Icon](https://www.inchcalculator.com/convert/flour/)
- [Salt Conversion CalculatorsChevron Right Icon](https://www.inchcalculator.com/convert/salt-volume/)
- [Sugar Conversion CalculatorsChevron Right Icon](https://www.inchcalculator.com/convert/sugar/)
- [Oven to Air Fryer Conversion CalculatorChevron Right Icon](https://www.inchcalculator.com/oven-to-air-fryer-conversion-calculator/)

[See All](https://www.inchcalculator.com/cooking-calculators/)

## References

1. The BC Cook Articulation Committee, Basic Kitchen and Food Service Management, https://ecampusontario.pressbooks.pub/basickitchenandfoodservicemanagement/chapter/convert-and-adjust-recipes-and-formulas/
2. National Institute of Standards & Technology, Culinary Measurement Tips, https://www.nist.gov/pml/owm/culinary-measurement-tips

### Help

[How to use the recipe converter](https://recipecard.io/recipe-converter/#) [Advanced Tips](https://recipecard.io/recipe-converter/#) [How do I save a conversion?](https://recipecard.io/recipe-converter/#)

### How to Use the Recipe Converter

1. Enter your recipe ingredients, one per line
2. Choose between multiplier mode or portion mode
3. Enter your conversion value and click convert
4. View your converted recipe with smart fraction rounding
5. Print or copy the converted recipe

### Recipe Converter Tutorial

The Recipe Converter helps you easily scale any recipe up or down. Perfect for adjusting serving sizes, batch cooking, or halving recipes.

#### Step by Step Guide:

1. **Enter Your Ingredients:** Type or paste your ingredient list into the text area. Each ingredient should be on its own line.
2. **Choose Conversion Method:**
   - **Multiplier:** Scale by a specific factor (2x, 0.5x, etc.)
   - **Portion:** Convert between serving sizes (4 servings to 6 servings)
3. **Smart Fractions:** Enable "Round to nearest fraction" for clean measurements like ½ cup instead of 0.5 cups.
4. **Convert & Use:** Click convert to see your scaled recipe. Print or copy for easy use in the kitchen.

### Advanced Tips

- **Fraction Rounding:** The converter rounds to the nearest 1/8th for practical cooking measurements
- **Quick Buttons:** Use the preset multiplier buttons for common conversions
- **Include Original:** Check "Include original recipe" to see side-by-side comparison
- **Batch Cooking:** Use the multiplier mode to easily scale up recipes for meal prep
- **Precise Measurements:** Disable fraction rounding for baking recipes that require exact measurements

### How do I save a conversion?

You can save your converted recipes in several ways:

- **Print:** Click the "Print Recipe" button to create a printable version
- **Copy to Clipboard:** Use the copy button to paste into your notes or recipe app
- **Browser Bookmark:** Bookmark this page to return to your conversions later

**Pro Tip:** For permanent recipe storage with nutrition analysis, try our
[Recipe Analyzer](https://recipecard.io/recipe-nutrition-analyzer/) tool which includes recipe saving features.


×

#### Login Required

You need to create a free account to save recipes. Your recipes will be safely stored and available whenever you log in.

Create an AccountSign in

## Powerful Features for Recipe Conversion

Advanced functionality and smart technology handle any scaling challenge with precision and ease, delivering a professional-grade recipe converter calculator that simplifies cooking for both home chefs and culinary professionals.

### Smart Fraction Conversion

Automatically converts decimal measurements to clean fractions, such as ½ cup or ¾ teaspoon, for easier cooking and measuring.

![Smart Fraction Conversion](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20570%20570'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

![Multiple Conversion Methods](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20570%20570'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

### Multiple Conversion Methods

Choose between multiplier scaling (2x, 0.5x) or portion-based conversion (4 servings to 6 servings) for maximum flexibility.

### Precise Measurements

Get accurate conversions for all ingredient types, including weight, volume, and count-based measurements, with professional precision.

![Precise Measurements](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20570%20570'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

![Save and Organize Your Recipes](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20570%20570'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

### Save and Organize Your Recipes

Never lose track of your favorite dishes again. Build your personal recipe library with smart organization tools: save analyzed recipes, create custom cookbooks, and access your collection from any device.

[Discover recipe organization](https://recipecard.io/recipe-nutrition-analyzer/organize-recipes/)

## Perfect Recipe Scaling Every Time

Transform your cooking experience with effortless recipe scaling that eliminates guesswork, saves precious time, reduces food waste, and gives you confidence when preparing meals for any number of people.

### Save Time in the Kitchen

Let our meal plan generator do the work. Set your calorie target, choose your diet type (Keto, Vegan, Mediterranean), and get a full week of personalized meal plans in seconds. No thinking required.

### Cook for Any Crowd Size

Prefer full control? Browse recipes from our database or use your saved recipes to create personalized meal plans. Choose exactly what you want to eat, when you want to eat it.

### Reduce Food Waste

See daily calorie totals and macros (protein, carbs, fat) at a glance. Every meal card displays complete nutritional information, so you can hit your health goals without manual calculations or spreadsheets.

## How to Use the Recipe Converter

Convert any recipe in just a few simple steps:

#1

### Enter Your Ingredients

Type or paste your ingredient list into the text area. Each ingredient should be listed on its own line, including quantities and measurements.

#2

### Choose Conversion Method

Select between multiplier scaling (2x, 0.5x, etc.) or portion conversion (changing from 4 servings to 6 servings).

#3

### Configure Settings

Enable “Round to nearest fraction” for clean measurements, such as ½ cup instead of 0.5 cups, for easier cooking.

#4

### Convert & Use

Click convert to see your perfectly scaled recipe. Print, copy, or save the results for easy use in your kitchen.

## Frequently Asked Questions

#### How do I format my ingredient list?

Simply type or paste your ingredient list into the text area with each ingredient on its own line. The format should include quantity and measurement (like “2 cups flour” or “1 tablespoon olive oil”). Our smart parser automatically handles various measurement formats.

* * *

#### What’s the difference between multiplier and portion conversion?

Multiplier conversion scales by a specific factor (like doubling with 2x or halving with 0.5x). You can use preset multiplier buttons for common conversions. Portion conversion allows you to specify the original and desired serving sizes (such as converting from 4 servings to 6 servings), and we calculate the appropriate multiplier automatically.

* * *

#### How does the smart fraction feature work?

The smart fraction feature converts decimal results to the nearest common cooking fraction, specifically rounding to the nearest 1/8th for practical cooking measurements (like ½, ⅓, ¼, ⅛). This provides cleaner, more practical results for home cooking. For baking recipes that require exact measurements, you can disable fraction rounding to get precise decimal values.

* * *

#### Can I see my original recipe and the converted one side by side?

Yes! Check the “Include original recipe” option to see a side-by-side comparison of your original recipe and the converted version. This helps you verify the scaling and builds confidence in the conversion accuracy.

* * *

#### How can I save or print my converted recipes?

You have several options to save your converted recipes: Click the “Print Recipe” button to create a printable version, use the copy button to paste the results into your notes or recipe app, or bookmark this page in your browser to return to your conversions later.

* * *

#### How accurate are the recipe conversions?

Our recipe conversion calculator uses precise mathematical calculations to scale ingredients proportionally. The conversions are highly accurate for volume, weight, and count-based measurements. However, cooking times and temperatures may require slight adjustments when scaling recipes significantly.

* * *

#### Can I convert recipes with mixed measurement units?

Yes! Our recipe size converter handles recipes with various measurement units, including cups, tablespoons, teaspoons, ounces, pounds, grams, and more. It will maintain the original unit format while scaling the quantities appropriately.

## Never Miss a New Feature!

![Never Miss New Features](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20384%2096'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

Join 27K+ users getting notified when we launch new recipe analysis capabilities and nutrition tools.

Join now

\*No spam, unsubscribe anytime

You have successfully joined our newsletter!

![.](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201%201'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

## Other Tools from Recipe Card Blocks

These powerful tools are designed to help you easily scale your recipes up or down for any serving size, instantly convert measurements between US and Metric units, and calculate your daily calorie needs for perfect results every time.

![Recipe Analyzer](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20328%20237'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

### Recipe Analyzer

Analyze nutrition facts for any recipe

[Start converting](https://recipecard.io/recipe-nutrition-analyzer/)

![Recipe Unit Converter](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20328%20209'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

### Recipe Unit Converter

Effortlessly convert recipe measurements between US and Metric systems.

[Start converting](https://recipecard.io/recipe-unit-converter/)

![Calorie Calculator](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20328%20226'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

### Calorie Calculator

Calculate your daily calorie needs and macronutrient targets based on your goals.

[Calculate calories](https://recipecard.io/calorie-calculator/)

web.markdown:
# Recipe Converter

## Resize Your Recipe

Multiply your recipes easily using our recipe converter tool. Fill out the information below and click Resize to get started.

Recipe Name

Notes

Original Recipe Serves

web.title: Recipe Converter Calculator - MyKitchenCalculator.com
web.description: A kitchen tool that can be used to help you multiply, divide, portion, or scale your recipes to a desired yield or serving size.
web.markdown:
### Support this website by downloading the Kitchen Calculator app

![Kitchen Calculator app icon](https://www.mykitchencalculator.com/images/MKC150.png)

[![Download on the Apple App Store](https://mykitchencalculator.com/images/apple_badge_100.png)](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/my-kitchen-calculator/id1552350111?itsct=apps_box_link&itscg=30200)[![Get it on Google Play](https://mykitchencalculator.com/images/google_play_badge_100.png)](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.mykitchencalculator.kitchencalculator&pcampaignid=pcampaignidMKT-Other-global-all-co-prtnr-py-PartBadge-Mar2515-1)

# Recipe Converter

[![Share on Facebook](https://mykitchencalculator.com/images/icons/facebook_square_32.png)](https://www.facebook.com/share.php?u=https://mykitchencalculator.com/recipeconverter.html)[![Share on Twitter](https://mykitchencalculator.com/images/icons/twitter_square_32.png)](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://mykitchencalculator.com/recipeconverter.html&text=Recipe%20Conversion%20made%20easy!)[![Share on Pinterest](https://mykitchencalculator.com/images/icons/pinterest_square_32.png)](https://www.pinterest.com/pin/create/button/?url=http%3A%2F%2Fmykitchencalculator.com%2Frecipeconverter.html&media=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.mykitchencalculator.com%2Fimages%2Fabout%2Frecipeconverter_og_600.png&description=One%20of%20the%20best%20online%20Recipe%20Converters%20for%20cooking%20and%20baking!)

Recipe Title


[Video Tutorial](https://mykitchencalculator.com/vhowtouserecipeconverter.html)

Ingredient List


Clear List

Round conversion to nearest cooking
fraction. (Nearest 1/8th)


Include original recipe in conversion.


Convert by Multiplier

Convert by Portion

Multiplier


× Multiply÷ Divide

Desired Size

[How to use the recipe\\
converter](https://mykitchencalculator.com/howtouserecipeconverter.html)

[Advanced Tips](https://mykitchencalculator.com/howtoadvancedtips.html)

[How do I save a\\
conversion?](https://mykitchencalculator.com/howtosaverecipeconversion.html)

### Do you have an Amazon Echo device?    Tell Alexa what to say and choose from over 30 voices to hear it in!

[![Enable Say What I Say](https://mykitchencalculator.com/images/swis_badge.png)](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B07C58Q6MK?pf_rd_r=1TQTFGDXC624ZP3N1MDT&pf_rd_p=edaba0ee-c2fe-4124-9f5d-b31d6b1bfbee)[![Visit MyAlexaTalks.com](https://mykitchencalculator.com/images/mat_badge.png)](https://myalexatalks.com/)

# About the Recipe Converter

The recipe converter (also known as a recipe calculator, recipe multiplier, recipe divider, recipe
scaler, or serving sizer)
is a kitchen tool that can be used to help you multiply, divide, portion, or scale your recipes to a
desired yield or serving size.
It's almost as easy as copy, paste, convert!


View the [video\\
tutorial](https://mykitchencalculator.com/vhowtouserecipeconverter.html), see the instructions below, or visit the [Tutorial](https://mykitchencalculator.com/howto.html) section for details on how to use the recipe converter.


You can also download the [MyKitchenCalculator.com App](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.mykitchencalculator.kitchencalculator&pcampaignid=pcampaignidMKT-Other-global-all-co-prtnr-py-PartBadge-Mar2515-1) (currently only available on
Android
and Kindle devices), which includes most of the conversion tools found on our website and
additional tools such as an ingredient unit converter that allows you to convert between
volume and weight measurements for more then 250 ingredients!


To hear about the latest updates to our site or app, follow our [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/mykitchencalculator) page.


# How to use the Recipe Converter

### (Recipe Calculator / Recipe Multiplier / Recipe Divider / Serving Sizer / Recipe Scaler)

![recipe converter](https://mykitchencalculator.com/images/about/recipeconverter600.jpg)

01. Enter the title of the recipe (optional)
02. Type in or copy and paste the ingredients section of a recipe into the ingredient list
     text area.




    Note:
    The copy and paste feature may not work properly for some recipe sites or food
    blogs due to the way the site formats their text. The copy and paste feature
    may also not work properly for certain mobile browsers. If this happens, the
    ingredients can be typed in or each line that was pasted into the text area
    can be edited. See below comments for details.


03. If necessary, edit the ingredients so that they are in standard recipe format and each
     word or number is separated by a space.



     (The standard recipe format for ingredients starts with the quantity, followed by the
     unit
     measure, and ends with the ingredient.)



    1 quart milk


04. Edit the recipe so that each ingredient line is on a new line. (New lines are created by
     pressing enter.)




    1 cup milk



    2 Tbs sugar



    1 tsp salt


05. Edit each line so that quantities and words are separated by a space.


06. Check the "Round conversion to nearest cooking fraction" box if you want the converted
     ingredient quantities to be rounded to the nearest cooking fraction. (Checked by
     default).






    Note:
    When rounding to nearest fraction, any quantities less than 1/8 will be rounded
    up to 1/8.


07. Check the "Include original recipe in conversion" box if you want to include the
     original
     recipe in the converted recipe text area. (Unchecked by default)

08. Click the corresponding tab to choose to convert by portion or by
     multiplier/divider.

09. Enter a recipe multiplier/divider, or enter an original size(serving/yield) and a
     desired
     size(serving/yield).

10. Press the multiply, divide, or portion button and the converted recipe will be displayed
     in the converted recipe text area.






    Note:
    Only numbers placed at the beginning of each line will be automatically
    converted. To convert other numbers that are on the same line, use the ##
    operator to indicate that you would like that number to be converted. To use
    the ## operator, place a ## in front of each number you would like to convert
    while leaving a space after that number. The ## operator can be placed in front
    of a number that is in decimal or fraction format.







    1 1/4 cup banana (about ##3 bananas)


    3/4 cup butter (##1 1/2 sticks)


    1 cup sugar (##200 g)


    1 pound ##5 ounces flour


    3 to ##4 eggs





     For more tips on how to use the ## operator, visit our [How to use scale\\
     operators](https://mykitchencalculator.com/howtousescaleoperator.html) section. See comments below for
     other details.


11. Check the converted recipe for correctness. If necessary, the converted recipe can be
     manually edited.

12. The converted recipe can be printed or copied and pasted into a document.



### Comments:

1. Fractions can be entered as a quantity. When using fractions, separate whole numbers
    from fractions with a space.




One-third = "1/3", one and one-half = "1 1/2".


2. Make sure to remove any bullet points that may appear before the quantity or at the
    start of each line.


3. Ingredients do not require a unit measure.




1 watermelon = "1 watermelon".


4. Ingredients that have a quantity range will not convert properly. To accomplish this,
    please see
    the note about using the "##" operator above.




"3 to 4 cups bananas" (multiplied by 2) will incorrectly be converted to: "6 to
4 cups
bananas"



"3-4 cups bananas" (multiplied by 2) will not be converted and will display the
original
text.


5. Always check the converted recipe for correctness.

6. Check to make sure the cooking/baking instructions are updated as a result of the
    conversion.



_expand\_less_

Top
